steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The passage describes artists who combine Indigenous music with other styles. Kalluk “incorporated” throat singing into electronic music. The word “Similarly” signals that Waban did the same kind of thing: merged powwow music with folk-rock. The blank needs a verb meaning to combine or mix two distinct musical styles into one work.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — confused: Means to mix up in a disordered or unclear way. The passage celebrates deliberate, skilled artistry — not accidental disorder. ✗
  • Choice B — replaced: Means to substitute one thing for another. Waban did not eliminate one genre in favor of the other; she combined both. ✗
  • Choice C — compared: Means to examine similarities and differences analytically. Waban is creating music, not conducting an analysis of two genres. ✗
  • Choice D — blended: Means to combine smoothly into a unified whole. This matches the passage’s description of fusing powwow and folk-rock into a single album, paralleling how Kalluk incorporated throat singing into electronic music. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
